parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: When 'On' data is captured from the Kinect sensor.

parameters.name: hwversion
parameters.label: Hardware Version
parameters.description: Choose between Kinect v1 or Kinect v2 sensors.

parameters.name: sensor
parameters.label: Sensor
parameters.description: Selects which Kinect sensor to use. Only available when using Kinect v1.

parameters.name: skeleton
parameters.label: Skeleton
parameters.description: Selects options for skeletal tracking.

parameters.name: maxplayers
parameters.label: Max Players
parameters.description: Limits how many players to track.

parameters.name: interactions
parameters.label: Interactions
parameters.description: Enables interactions, which returns interaction data such as "grip" and "press".  The additional channels output are prefixed with and .<

parameters.name: relbonerotations
parameters.label: Relative Bone Rotations
parameters.description: Returns rx, ry, and rz relative rotation channels for each bone. The rotation is relative to the previous joint.

parameters.name: absbonerotations
parameters.label: Absolute Bone Rotations
parameters.description: Returns rx, ry, and rz absolute rotation channels for each bone.

parameters.name: bonelengths
parameters.label: Bone Lengths
parameters.description: Returns the length for each bone.

parameters.name: unrollbones
parameters.label: Unroll Bone Values
parameters.description: Return the rx, ry, and rz values in a form that includes no discontinuities (or wrap arounds), so that they can be safely blended, filtered, lagged etc. For example, a rotation value running from 0 to

parameters.name: worldspace
parameters.label: World Space Positions
parameters.description: Returns the tracked positions in world space coordinates. For each tracked point, a , , triplet of channels will be output.

parameters.name: colorspace
parameters.label: Color Space Positions
parameters.description: Returns the tracked positions in image space coordinates relative to the color camera. For each tracked point, a , , triplet of channels will be output. Note this is only available on Kinectv2.

parameters.name: depthspace
parameters.label: Depth Space Positions
parameters.description: Returns the tracked positions in image space coordinates relative to the depth camera. For each tracked point, a , , triplet of channels will be output. Works on both Kinectv1 and Kinectv2.

parameters.name: facetracking
parameters.label: Face Tracking
parameters.description: Returns face tracking channels for detected faces.

parameters.name: statuschans
parameters.label: Status Channels
parameters.description: A number of additional status channels are reported when this is turned on. A group of channels will report if a joint is currently being tracked, and another group of channels reports if part of the

parameters.name: neardepthmode
parameters.label: Near Depth Mode
parameters.description: Enables near mode for skeleton tracking, which allows the depth camera to see objects as close as 40cm to the camera (instead of the default 80cm).

parameters.name: flipskelu
parameters.label: Flip Skeleton U Direction
parameters.description: Flips the u-axis for skeleton channels, helpful when using a mirror image from the camera.

parameters.name: flipfaceu
parameters.label: Flip Face U Direction
parameters.description: Flips the u-axis for face channels, helpful when using a mirror image from the camera.

parameters.name: jointsmoothing
parameters.label: Joint Smoothing
parameters.description: Activates Kinect's smoothing algorithm for joint tracking and enables the smoothing parameters below.

parameters.name: smoothing
parameters.label: Smoothing
parameters.description: Increasing the smoothing parameter value leads to more highly-smoothed skeleton position values being returned. It is the nature of smoothing that, as the smoothing value is increased, responsiveness

parameters.name: correction
parameters.label: Correction
parameters.description: Lower values are slower to correct towards the raw data and appear smoother, while higher values will correct toward the raw data more quickly. Values must be in the range 0 through 1.0.

parameters.name: prediction
parameters.label: Prediction
parameters.description: The number of frames to predict into the future. Values must be greater than or equal to zero. Values greater than 0.5 will likely lead to overshooting when moving quickly. This effect can be damped b

parameters.name: jitterrad
parameters.label: Jitter Radius
parameters.description: The radius in meters for jitter reduction. Any jitter beyond this radius is clamped to the radius.

parameters.name: maxdevrad
parameters.label: Max Deviation Radius
parameters.description: The maximum radius in meters that filtered positions are allowed to deviate from raw data. Filtered values that would be more than this radius from the raw data are clamped at this distance, in the di

parameters.name: rotationsmoothing
parameters.label: Rotation Smoothing
parameters.description: Activates Kinect's smoothing algorithm for rotations. As with joint smoothing above, higher levels of smoothing will introduce more latency.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).
